title: SSO login redirect loop: Entra ID email claims don't match TaskFlow user records (plus-addressing stripped, aliased emails differ)
problem: After migrating from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, ~30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop during login. Entra ID returns email claims that differ from what TaskFlow has stored — specifically, plus-addressed emails have the plus part stripped, and users with aliased/changed emails get a different primary email than what's on file. TaskFlow fails to match the identity and redirects back to SSO.
root_cause_hypothesis: TaskFlow's auth callback does a strict match between the email claim in the OIDC token and the stored user email. Entra ID normalizes emails differently than Okta: it strips plus-addressing (jane+taskflow@company.com → jane@company.com) and may return a different primary email for users with directory aliases. The strict match fails, so TaskFlow treats the user as unauthenticated and re-initiates the SSO flow. Stale Okta session cookies may compound the issue for some users.
reproduction_steps: Create a TaskFlow user with a plus-addressed email (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com)
Configure Entra ID as the SSO provider with the user's primary email set to jane@company.com
Attempt to log into TaskFlow via SSO
Observe that Entra ID returns jane@company.com in the email claim
TaskFlow fails to match this to the stored jane+taskflow@company.com and redirects back to SSO
environment: TaskFlow with SSO authentication, recently migrated from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. Multiple browsers affected (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). Issue is identity-provider-level, not browser-specific.
impact: ~30% of users are completely unable to log into TaskFlow. Workarounds (incognito mode, clearing cookies) are unreliable and temporary.
recommended_fix: 1. Update TaskFlow's auth callback to normalize email comparison — strip plus-addressing and compare case-insensitively, or look up users by multiple email fields. 2. Consider matching on a stable claim like `sub` (subject ID) or `oid` (Entra object ID) rather than email, which is inherently mutable. 3. Add a one-time migration step to update stored user emails to match what Entra ID returns, or add an email-alias lookup table. 4. Invalidate pre-migration Okta session cookies to eliminate stale-session interference.
proposed_test_case: Test SSO login with a user whose TaskFlow-stored email is jane+taskflow@company.com but whose Entra ID email claim returns jane@company.com. Verify the user is successfully authenticated and matched to their TaskFlow account without a redirect loop.
information_gaps: Exact identity claim TaskFlow uses for user matching (email vs. sub vs. UPN) — determinable from code inspection
Whether the non-plus-addressed affected users' stored emails differ from their Entra ID primary email in a specific pattern — the aliasing explanation is plausible but unconfirmed for all cases
